steps.title: Note that angles K and L are complementary
steps.content: Triangle JKL has $\angle J = 90^\circ$, $\angle K = 71^\circ$, $\angle L = 19^\circ$. The two acute angles in any right triangle sum to $90^\circ$, and indeed $71^\circ + 19^\circ = 90^\circ$, so $K$ and $L$ are complementary.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content: For complementary angles, $\sin(\theta) = \cos(90^\circ - \theta)$. Since $L = 90^\circ - K$, we get $\cos L = \cos(90^\circ - K) = \sin K$. Numerically, $\sin 71^\circ \approx 0.9455$ and $\cos 19^\circ \approx 0.9455$ — they are equal.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $\cos L > \sin K$ — false; the values are equal, not greater. Eliminate.
  • B) $\cos L = \sin K$ — true by the cofunction identity. Keep.
  • C) $\cos L < \sin K$ — false; the values are equal, not less. Eliminate.
  • D) There is enough information; both angle measures are given. Eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
